response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software developer with strong web, backend, and some embedded experience, but the resume shows no exposure to SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator architecture, GPU workload analysis, deep neural network optimization, SIMD/MIMD design, or hardware-software co-design. The role is a Principal-level hardware performance architect position, and the candidate's background is almost entirely application-layer software engineering. The gap between the required hardware/AI-accelerator expertise and the candidate's demonstrated experience is too large to bridge through transferable skills.
strengths: Long track record (10+ years) of senior software engineering across multiple stacks
Some low-level C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and performance-focused DLang work at Sociomantic
Demonstrated ability to learn diverse technologies and contribute across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded domains
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience, and no hardware architecture background
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep neural network training/inference optimization work
No evidence of performance analysis at the hardware/microarchitecture level required for this role

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator or deep learning workload optimization background
